title: Migration, Crime and Education Analytics Project
text:
This project analyzes the relationship between internal migration, crime statistics, and education levels across Turkish provinces. Using data from TÜİK (Turkish Statistical Institute) covering the period 2018–2024, we aim to uncover socio-economic patterns that drive provincial differences in Turkey.
The study focuses on three main questions:

How does net migration vary across provinces, and which provinces attract or lose population?
Is there a relationship between migration patterns and crime indicators?
How are education levels related to migration and crime across provinces?

title: Migration, Crime and Education Analytics Project
text:
This project analyzes the relationship between internal migration, crime statistics, and education levels across Turkish provinces. Using data from TÜİK (Turkish Statistical Institute) covering the period 2018–2024, we aim to uncover socio-economic patterns that drive provincial differences in Turkey.
The study focuses on three main questions:

How does net migration vary across provinces, and which provinces attract or lose population?
Is there a relationship between migration patterns and crime indicators?
How are education levels related to migration and crime across provinces?

title: Migration, Crime and Education Analytics Project
text:
Team Members

Alp Çağrı Karapıçak
Koray Ekici
Habibe Akbaş
Mevlüt Faruk Dikmen
Yahya Eren Kaya

title: Migration, Crime and Education Analytics Project
text:
Data
All datasets are sourced from TÜİK and cover Turkish provinces from 2018 onwards (the period where all datasets align):

Provincial in-migration by reason (2018–2024)
Provincial out-migration by reason (2018–2024)
Educational attainment by province (2018–2024)
Convicted persons in correctional institutions by province

title: Migration, Crime and Education Analytics Project
text:
Methods

Net migration calculation (in-migration − out-migration) by province and year
Exploratory data analysis and visualization
Correlation analysis between migration, education, and crime variables
Linear regression models
Time series analysis (ARIMA/SARIMA) for migration trends
Heatmap visualizations for provincial comparisons

title: Migration, Crime and Education Analytics Project
text:
Tools

R (dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, readxl, forecast)
Quarto for reporting

title: Analysis
text:
Analysis results will be added here in subsequent project phases.
Planned analyses:

Net Migration Analysis — Calculate net migration (in − out) per province per year; identify top receiving and losing provinces
Correlation Analysis — Explore pairwise correlations between migration, education, and crime variables
Linear Regression — Model crime or migration as a function of education indicators
Time Series Forecasting — Apply ARIMA/SARIMA models to provincial migration trends (2018–2024)
Heatmap Visualizations — Provincial comparison maps for education levels and crime indicators

title: Data
text: All datasets are obtained from TÜİK (Türkiye İstatistik Kurumu — Turkish Statistical Institute). The analyses focus on the period 2018–2024, which is the overlapping timeframe for all datasets.

title: Data
text: All datasets are obtained from TÜİK (Türkiye İstatistik Kurumu — Turkish Statistical Institute). The analyses focus on the period 2018–2024, which is the overlapping timeframe for all datasets.

title: Data
text:
Datasets

1. Provincial In-Migration by Reason (aldigi_goc.xls)
Records the number of people who migrated into each province, broken down by reason for migration (work, education, family, housing, etc.).

Sheet: t10
Coverage: 81 provinces × 2018–2024
Key columns: Year, Province, Total in-migration, and migration reason categories



2. Provincial Out-Migration by Reason (verdigi_goc.xls)
Records the number of people who migrated out of each province, broken down by reason for migration.

Sheet: t11
Coverage: 81 provinces × 2018–2024
Key columns: Year, Province, Total out-migration, and migration reason categories



3. Education Status by Province (egitim.xlsx)
Population aged 6 and above classified by educational attainment level, broken down by province, year, and gender.

Sheet: Eğitim Durumu
Coverage: 81 provinces × multiple years (2018–2024)
Key columns: Year, Province, Education level, Total, Male, Female, Male %, Female %



4. Crime Data — Convicted Persons by Province (suc.xls)
Number of convicted persons entering correctional institutions, broken down by province.

Coverage: Multiple years including 2018 onwards
Key columns: Province (columns), Year (rows), Count of convicted persons

title: Data
text:
Data Preview

library(readxl)
library(knitr)

# Read by sheet index to avoid encoding issues across platforms
aldigi_goc  <- read_excel("data/aldigi_goc.xls",  sheet = 1)
verdigi_goc <- read_excel("data/verdigi_goc.xls", sheet = 1)
egitim      <- read_excel("data/egitim.xlsx",      sheet = 1)
suc         <- read_excel("data/suc.xls",          sheet = 1)
